parsed_review.summary: CP00-301 is a genuine descriptor-only CRM service-slice pack covering exactly the 10 planned RP09.P02.M07 "Test And Golden Case Set" units (S11-S20). The closeout-pack-plan.json entry (line 410), review-baseline.json, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and registry.js CP301 binding all agree: risk A, unit_count 10, range RP09.P02.M07.S11-RP09.P02.M07.S20, upstream CP00-300, next CP00-302 / RP09.P02.M07.S21. CRM_CORE_CP301_REQUIREMENTS distributions match the prompt exactly (deliverable ui:2, implementation:3, claude_review:1, failure_recovery:2, test:2; micro_title "Test And Golden Case Set":10), and the single M07 section enumerates all 10 required row titles (lock acquisition rule, persistence boundary, validation error mapping, review/approval-required routing, blocked-claim output, rollback/retry behavior, happy/denied unit tests) as frozen descriptor-only rows. service.js/validators.js/registry.js produce only inert Object.freeze descriptors with every runtime/write/leak flag false; a source sweep of packages/crm and scripts/validate-rp09-crm-core-contract.mjs found no writeFile/appendFile/fetch/http/child_process/exec/spawn/SQL/process.env usage - only read-only readFile/readFileSync. model.test.js and the contract validator assert descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false, acquires_runtime_lock=false, performs_rollback/retry_runtime=false, denied-path outcome "denied_customer_safe", no-write attestations (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false), authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, and handoff to CP00-302/RP09.P02.M07.S21; command-evidence.json records all required implementation/contract/plan/repo-wide validations at exit 0. The manifest correctly keeps production_ready=false (status implemented_pending_review) instead of asserting enterprise trust from local validation, honoring the never-weaken-production_ready gate. Hermes appears only as deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptor metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime, and Claude's read-only / non-final-approver boundary holds across all artifacts. The readiness auth-preflight passed with non-empty valid-JSON stdout (no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed, fenced, or tool-call-shaped evidence). No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 documents that the post-review closeout artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet on disk, which is expected because they are generated by post-review hardening steps after this read-only review.

findings.title: Post-review closeout artifacts not yet present (expected at this hardening step)
findings.details: manifest.json required_pack_artifacts and every unit's evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-301/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in that directory. This is consistent with the designed flow: pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence places finding_adjudication and construction_inspection after read_only_claude_run_raw_capture (the current step), and command-evidence.json explicitly marks closeout-pack:review:normalize/receipt:validate and closeout-pack:validate as post-review gated commands executed before commit. invalid_attempts_preserved is empty and the readiness report shows review_attempt_counted=false, so this is a clean first review attempt rather than a missing-evidence defect.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this read-only review step. Before commit, confirm the final closeout gate (npm run closeout-pack:validate) fails closed when any of the three artifacts are absent or invalid, so the manifest's required_pack_artifacts list is enforced rather than advisory.
